project.phase: Second Pass (Voice-Locked) — Phase 22+ ingestion (formula bank + particle stabilization)
project.origin: Open, reproducible Meroitic lexicon built from the Lackadaisical Security phase corpus (inscriptional convergence + morphology + formula structure).
project.origin_notes: This lexicon keeps polysemy explicit (context-dependent meanings are recorded, not flattened). Titles and kinship markers are treated as formulaic anchors; cultural interpretations are only promoted when they recur across inscriptions.

historical_context.period: Kush / Meroitic-era inscriptions (broad: 1st millennium BCE – mid 1st millennium CE)
historical_context.confidence_model: Per-entry confidence_score (0–1); overall status tracked via phase synthesis

principles: No-cherry-picking: promotion requires multi-attestation across independent contexts
Polysemy allowed only with independent recurrence per sense
Bound-morpheme entries permitted (e.g., -li) to prevent homograph collapse

migration.notes: Added Phase 26/27 second-pass entries (apote, qo, -li, rmn) and promoted areme->STABLE with Arome variants; preserved multi-sense + no-drift rules. Added small normalization variants (meroe, imn, kd) for corpus matching.

changes: Integrated PhaseZZ corpus validation: locked di 'give/offer' and mds 'descendant' at 0.75 confidence.
Reaffirmed qore hard-rule: never gloss as king; kingship requires mlo.
No new lemmas added beyond v12; PhaseZZ confirms remaining unknowns are proper names.
Raised di confidence to 0.90 (LOCKED) per integrity-check attestation review
Flagged/downgraded 'afterlife iron fields' to speculative interpretive layer (0.20)
Added explicit anti-drift notes for qore (never king by default)
Added conflict note for areme regarding conservative vs LS STABLE treatment

Based on meroitic_complete_script_MASTER-2026-02-24-v14_LS_multisense.json. Updated kndke: clarified Kandake as Queen/Mother-Queen and highest feminine royal authority; added anti-drift sovereignty notes.

attestations: context: Royal protocols (after personal names, denoting kingship)[5]; Offering formulas (“good” meal)[23] – an epithet of excellence tied to kings and gods.
source: Meroitic Decipherment – Phases 6–7_ Cultural Pattern and Statistical Validation.docx
context: Reigning monarch; often paired with “of Kush” (kdi)[18]. Seen in royal names, temple dedicatory texts, etc. Implies divine sanction[29].
source: Phase 10 Synthesis_ Meroitic Decipherment Project.docx

cultural_context.sacred_kingship: Represents divine aspect of kingship beyond temporal power
cultural_context.matrilineal_context: Often associated with maternal lineage legitimacy

attestations: context: Royal protocols (after personal names, denoting kingship)[5]; Offering formulas (“good” meal)[23] – an epithet of excellence tied to kings and gods.
source: Meroitic Decipherment – Phases 6–7_ Cultural Pattern and Statistical Validation.docx
context: Reigning monarch; often paired with “of Kush” (kdi)[18]. Seen in royal names, temple dedicatory texts, etc. Implies divine sanction[29].

senses.definition: Crown prince / heir apparent / regional lord (non-sovereign)
attestations: context: Royal dedicatory texts (often preceding personal names of ruling queens or princes)[5]; Labels of subordinate rulers (e.g. “Nubian ruler” in tribute scene)[30].
source: Meroitic Decipherment – Phases 6–7_ Cultural Pattern and Statistical Validation.docx
context: Secondary royal title; crown prince or regional ruler[33]. Used in succession lists and royal family descriptions. Below mlo in hierarchy[48].
source: Phase 10 Synthesis_ Meroitic Decipherment Project.docx
source: Meroitic Decipherment Methodology_ Phase-by-Phase Analysis.docx
senses.notes: HARD RULE (LS): qore ≠ king. Never gloss as 'king/monarch/sovereign ruler'. If sovereign authority is encoded, the corpus uses mlo. When qore precedes later-king names, treat as pre-accession heir title or delegated/regional rulership, not kingship. FORBIDDEN: king/monarch/sovereign-ruler gloss by default; sovereignty requires mlo.

cultural_context.egyptian_influence: Demonstrates direct adoption of Egyptian royal titulary
cultural_context.succession: Often designates the chosen successor or co-ruler
cultural_context.note: Default gloss is 'prince/heir' in LS reading; avoid blanket 'king/ruler' unless context explicitly indicates co-regency.

entries.conflict_notes: Constraint: qore never equals mlo (king). Any translation or phase text that renders `qore` as 'king' is a mis-specification; correct to heir/prince/regional lord.

attestations: context: Royal dedicatory texts (often preceding personal names of ruling queens or princes)[5]; Labels of subordinate kings (e.g. “Nubian king” in tribute scene)[30].
source: Meroitic Decipherment – Phases 6–7_ Cultural Pattern and Statistical Validation.docx
context: Secondary royal title; crown prince or regional ruler[33]. Used in succession lists and royal family descriptions. Below mlo in hierarchy[48].

translations: water (SACRED ONLY), life force, consciousness flow

cultural_context.revolutionary_discovery: Only script with purely sacred water term
cultural_context.unique_feature: Complete separation of sacred/practical water terminology
cultural_context.consciousness_technology: Represents consciousness flow rather than physical water

senses.definition: Go / come / travel (literal motion; core verb).
senses.notes: Do not over-define metaphysics; translate the motion/transition function and let worldview implications remain hypothesis unless explicitly encoded.

definitions: Go / come / travel (literal motion; core verb).
In funerary formulas, marks a transition/journey; interpret with the surrounding cluster (e.g., with imnt).
Additional symbolic readings may be proposed when they emerge repeatedly, but should be labeled as inference rather than hard translation.

cultural_context.sacred_number: Three is a sacred number (e.g., triads of gods, triple phrases)
cultural_context.writing_system: Three strokes sometimes ligatured into a single glyph

cultural_context.completion: Ten often symbolizes completion or perfection in texts
cultural_context.egyptian_parallels: Parallel to Egyptian use of a single glyph for 10

definitions: Completeness (symbolic meaning of a full set)

senses.definition: Kandake — Queen / Mother-Queen (highest feminine royal authority of Kush; often sovereign co-ruler/regent)
attestations: Royal titulary of queens (e.g., Amanirenas, Amanishakheto)
context: Royal women’s tomb texts (title for reigning queen or king’s mother)[15]. Likely appears as kdikw or kdiw (“female ruler”), derived from kdi (“woman, sister”).
source: Meroitic Decipherment – Phases 6–7_ Cultural Pattern and Statistical Validation.docx
context: Title for reigning queen or queen-mother[43]. Appears with female names in royal contexts. Signifies independent female authority (often co-ruler or regent).
source: Phase 10 Synthesis_ Meroitic Decipherment Project.docx
source: Phase 11_ Advanced Kushite Morphology & Astronomy.docx
senses.notes: LS: treat as top-tier feminine sovereignty marker; avoid downgrading to mere consort.

cultural_context.feminine_authority: Special status for maternal authority roles
cultural_context.sovereign_authority: Highest feminine royal authority marker; may indicate reigning queen, queen-mother, or regent/co-ruler depending on context.
cultural_context.avoid_drift_note: Do not treat as generic 'queen' without sovereignty implications when in royal titulary slot.

attestations: Royal titulary of queens (e.g., Amanirenas, Amanishakheto)

cultural_context.cosmic_origin: Reflects a Kushite creation concept parallel to Egyptian Nun (primeval waters), but tied specifically to 'black soil' of Kush
cultural_context.consciousness_creation: The merging of black land and sacred water as source of life, aligning with 'consciousness flow' idea

senses.definition: Fixed funerary formula meaning a westward transition/journey (often used as a death euphemism).
senses.notes: Voice-lock: translate the formula, not theology.

cultural_context.euphemism: Culturally preferred expression for death, avoiding blunt language
cultural_context.egyptian_parallel: Direct parallel to Egyptian concept of 'west' as realm of the dead, showing conceptual syncretism

definitions: Fixed funerary formula meaning a westward transition/journey (often used as a death euphemism).
Translate as the formula; do not expand into doctrine unless the inscription itself adds details.
If additional imagery (metals/craft/fields/etc.) appears elsewhere, treat it as emergent hypothesis tied to attested motifs.

cultural_context.titular_tradition: Defines the format of Kushite royal names, analogous to Egyptian 'King of Upper and Lower Egypt, [Name]'
cultural_context.legitimacy: By including 'Kush', emphasizes the king's domain and sacred land in the title

cultural_context.libation_rite: Key formula in daily temple ritual, equivalent to Egyptian 'Htp-di-nsw' concept but specific (water offering)
cultural_context.oral_performance: Probably chanted repeatedly by priests while pouring libations

attestations: Royal and noble genealogies on stelae

morphology.syntax: Personal name + lineage marker + parent name

cultural_context.dynastic_legitimacy: Establishes identity and legitimacy through parentage (especially important for kings listing mothers or fathers)
cultural_context.language_feature: Shows use of a post-positional genitive, a grammatical feature aligning with Nubian languages

attestations: Royal and noble genealogies on stelae

cultural_context.symmetry_magic: Repetition of the name around 'eternity' believed to magically ensure the perpetual existence of the person
cultural_context.inscription_style: Unique Kushite stylistic device, perhaps chanted in echo: name...djet...name

attestations: Protection spells calling on the land

cultural_context.resonance_invoke: Believed to generate a protective or empowering field through repetition (possibly chanted loud in ceremonies)
cultural_context.national_consciousness: Functions as an affirmation of national identity and unity, reinforcing the 'field' of Kush in ritual

definitions: Kush (as an invocation of the land/people in mantra form)
attestations: Protection spells calling on the land
